question_title: Will a wildfire-related death be reported in Turkey between June 18 and June 28, 2026?
summary: Turkey has a historical base rate of ~15% for having a wildfire-related death in any given June 18-28 window (1 of 6 years, 2020-2025). Current conditions are somewhat elevated: the question describes active wildfires near Antalya with evacuations, a building African heatwave peaking June 23-29 at 38-40°C (overlapping the window perfectly), and strong winds. However, important mitigating factors include exceptionally wet antecedent conditions in 2026 (137-231% of normal precipitation), the fundamental rarity of wildfire deaths (even with thousands of fires, deaths are rare episodic events requiring specific unlucky circumstances like wind shifts), and no deaths reported in Turkey yet in June 2026. The broad definition of death (including heart attacks, vehicle accidents while fleeing) slightly expands the probability. I estimate 23%, reflecting a moderate upward update from the 15% base for the elevated heat and fire conditions, tempered by the wet season and episodic rarity of deaths. My confidence is low (0.35) given the tiny historical sample (6 years) and inability to independently verify current fire conditions described in the question.

factors.description: Active wildfires reported near Antalya (Kundu, Belek, Aksu areas) per question description, with hotel/residential evacuations - though independently unverified by news search
African heatwave with peak temperatures of 38-40°C forecast for June 23-29 (Diyarbakir 39.6°C, Antalya 38.2°C) - overlaps almost perfectly with question window
Strong winds reported as ongoing, which are the primary mechanism for catastrophic firefighter deaths in Turkey (wind shift killed 10 in Eskisehir July 2025)
Exceptionally wet antecedent conditions in Jan-Jun 2026 (137-231% of normal precipitation across Turkey) reduce early-season fuel dryness
Deaths are episodic and extremely rare per-fire: ~1,459 fires in June 2025 produced only 1 death event (3 deaths); 6,800 fires in all of 2025 produced only ~18 deaths total
Recent trend toward June death events: both 2024 (June 21, in window) and 2025 (June 29, just outside) had deadly June fires - first such events in the dataset

reasoning:
## Forecast Summary

**Question:** Will a wildfire-related death be reported in Turkey between June 18 and June 28, 2026?

**Probability: 23%**

### Key Analysis

**Base rate (~15%):** In 6 years of data (2020-2025), exactly 1 year had a wildfire death in the June 18-28 window — the 2024 Diyarbakır fire (~June 21). Poisson modeling of death-event rates gives 11-14%.

**Upward factors (+8pp):**
- Active wildfires already reported near Antalya with evacuations
- African heatwave peaking June 23-29 at 38-40°C — overlapping the window almost perfectly
- Strong winds (the #1 mechanism for firefighter deaths)
- 2024 and 2025 both had June death events (first time in the dataset)

**Downward factors (partially offsetting):**
- Exceptionally wet 2026 (137-231% of normal rainfall) reduces early-season fuel
- Deaths are episodically rare: ~1,459 fires in June 2025 → only 1 death event
- No deaths reported yet in June 2026

**Confidence: Low (0.35)** — Tiny historical sample (6 years), inability to independently verify current Antalya fire conditions, and the inherently episodic nature of wildfire deaths make this estimate uncertain within ±10pp.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will a wildfire-related death be reported in Turkey between June 18 and June 28, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/44066)
[Turkiye: Wildfire Breaks Out in Kundu Area of Antalya | Sada Elbalad](https://see.news/turkiye-wildfire-breaks-out-in-kundu-area-of-antalya)

[Flames approach five-star hotel resort after huge forest fire breaks out](https://www.nationalworld.com/europe/titanic-deluxe-belek-fire-very-smokey-around-turkey-five-star-hotel-resort-after-large-forest-fire-antalya-5327769)
[Devastating fire rips through holiday hotspot with tourists forced to evacuate hotels](https://www.gbnews.com/news/world/turkey-fires-antalya-holiday-hotspor-tourists-evacuate-hotels)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves YES if at least one human death is reported as caused by a wildfire (an uncontrolled vegetation fire, including forest, brush, or grassland fires) anywhere in Turkey, where the fire occurred and the death happened between 00:00 on June 18, 2026 and 23:59 on June 28, 2026 local Turkish time (TRT, UTC+3), as reported by a Turkish government authority (such as AFAD or the Ministry of Agriculture and Forestry) or by [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions). Deaths of firefighters, forestry workers, volunteers, residents, or tourists all count, including deaths from burns, smoke inhalation, or other direct medical events (e.g., heart attack or vehicle accident) sustained while fighting or fleeing a qualifying wildfire. One or more such reported deaths resolves YES; zero resolves NO.
fine_print: "Wildfire" excludes structure fires, industrial fires, and vehicle fires that are not part of an uncontrolled vegetation fire. The death must be reported as wildfire-related by the qualifying sources; an unexplained death merely occurring near a fire does not count unless attributed to the fire. If a person is injured by a qualifying wildfire during the window but dies after June 28, 2026, the question still resolves YES provided the fire and injury occurred within the window and the death is attributed to that fire by a qualifying source before the resolution date. If no qualifying source reports any wildfire-related death meeting these conditions by the resolution date, the question resolves NO.

factors.description: Active wildfires already burning near Antalya (Kundu, Belek, Aksu areas) with hotel and residential evacuations underway as of mid-June 2026
African heatwave building with peak temperatures of 38-40°C forecast for June 23-29 - overlaps almost perfectly with the question window
Strong winds reported, which are the primary cause of firefighter deaths in Turkey (wind shifts trapped and killed 10 in 2025 Eskisehir fire)
Exceptionally wet antecedent conditions in 2026 (137-231% of normal precipitation) reduce early-season fuel dryness and fire severity
Deaths are episodic and rare even in active fire years - Turkey had ~1,459 fires in June 2025 but only 3 deaths (one event on June 29), and 6,800 fires total in 2025 with only 17 deaths
Trend toward more severe fire seasons: 2024 and 2025 both had June death events (2024: June 21 in window; 2025: June 29 just outside), climate change extending fire season

input.anchor: Historical base rate: ~15% (1 of 6 years 2020-2025 had a wildfire death event in the exact June 18-28 window; Poisson model gives 11-14%)
input.assessment:
The question asks whether at least one wildfire-related death will be reported in Turkey in an 11-day window (June 18-28, 2026). 

Historical base rate: Looking at 2020-2025 data, Turkey had wildfire deaths in June in 2 of 6 years (2024: 12 deaths around June 21; 2025: 3 deaths on June 29). Only the 2024 event (June 21) falls within the June 18-28 window, giving a raw frequency of 1/6 = 16.7%. Poisson modeling of death-event rates gives 11-14%.

Current conditions are elevated vs. average: The question description reports active wildfires near Antalya with evacuations, an African heatwave, and strong winds. A heat pulse peaking June 23-29 overlaps perfectly with the question window. This resembles 2024 and 2025 more than quiet years (2020, 2022, 2023).

Key mitigating factors: (1) 2026 had exceptional precipitation (137-231% of normal), reducing fuel dryness early in the season. (2) Deaths are extremely rare per-fire - even in 2025 with 1,459 June fires, only one death-event occurred. (3) No deaths have been reported in Turkey yet in June 2026. (4) The research sub-agent could not independently confirm the June 2026 Antalya fires described in the question.

Key risk factors: (1) Active fires already burning. (2) Strong winds - the primary mechanism for firefighter deaths. (3) Heat intensifying into the window period. (4) Last two years both had June deaths, suggesting elevated baseline.

I estimate ~25% probability, reflecting elevated conditions relative to the ~15% base rate but acknowledging that deaths remain rare episodic events even amid active fire activity.
input.calibration_notes: Anchor: ~15% from 1/6 historical frequency. Current estimate of 25% represents a moderate upward update from the base. The estimate doesn't seem hedged toward 50% - it takes a clear position that NO is more likely than YES, which is appropriate given the episodic rarity of deaths. The 2024 event (June 21, in window) and 2025 event (June 29, barely outside) bracket the window nicely.
input.key_uncertainties: 1. Whether the fires described in the question background are actually occurring right now or are speculative framing. 2. How much the wet antecedent conditions mitigate early-season risk given the heatwave. 3. The very small sample size (6 years) makes any base rate estimate noisy. 4. Whether current conditions will produce the specific unlucky circumstances (wind shifts, trapped crews) that cause deaths.
input.tool_audit: Research sub-agent provided excellent historical death data by year and month (2020-2025). It also confirmed no June 2026 deaths have been reported yet. Web search found articles about Kundu/Belek fires but dates were ambiguous - some appear to be September 2025. The temperature/heatwave research was very useful with specific forecast data. Monte Carlo code execution worked well for base rate calculations.
input.update_triggers: - Report of a wildfire death in Turkey between now and June 28 (immediate resolution to YES). - A major wildfire blowup in the Antalya/Aegean region during the forecast window would push toward 50%+. - Rain or wind shift bringing cooler/wetter conditions would push toward 10-15%.
input.process_reflection: The research tool was excellent for gathering historical data. The main friction was in confirming current conditions - the question description describes specific ongoing fires and evacuations, but the research sub-agent couldn't confirm them from news sources. This could be a recency issue (fires too recent to be indexed) or the question may have been written with speculative framing. The web search also returned many 2021 and 2025 results mixed in with potentially 2026 ones, making it hard to determine dates for some articles. A tool that could parse article dates more reliably would help.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
## Checks Performed

### Resolution Criteria
✅ Present and fully engaged with. The agent fetched the Metaculus question (post_id 44066) and the factors directly address the specific terms: wildfire deaths in Turkey, June 18-28 window, AFAD/credible source reporting.

### Factor-by-Factor Verification

**Factor 1 (Active wildfires near Antalya, logit=+0.5, conf=0.7)** — **PARTIALLY UNSUPPORTED.** The agent's own research sub-agent explicitly concluded: "No evidence of major active wildfires, deaths, or evacuations in Antalya or Turkey in June 2026 as of June 16, 2026." The see.news article about a Kundu fire had no date. The GBNews and Nationalworld articles were identified by the agent itself as being about September 2025, not June 2026. The agent is relying on the *Metaculus question background text* as its source for this factor, not independently verified news. The agent acknowledges this discrepancy in its thinking (lines 782-789, 802-803) and takes "the question description at face value." While this is a reasonable judgment call (question authors typically check current conditions), the factor presents unverified claims as established fact.

**Factor 2 (Heatwave 38-40°C, logit=+0.5, conf=0.6)** — ✅ Grounded. Open-Meteo forecast data in the research shows Diyarbakir 39.6°C (Jun 27), Antalya 38.2°C (Jun 29), Adana 38.0°C (Jun 29).

**Factor 3 (Strong winds, Eskisehir 10 killed, logit=+0.4, conf=0.6)** — ✅ Grounded. Research confirms 10 killed in Seyitgazi/Eskisehir in July 2025 when wind shifted. Current wind conditions mentioned in multiple search results.

**Factor 4 (Wet conditions, logit=-0.5, conf=0.7)** — ✅ Grounded. Precipitation data from Open-Meteo: Adana 231%, Ankara 170%, Antalya 153%, Istanbul 137% of normal.

**Factor 5 (Deaths episodic/rare, logit=-0.3, conf=0.8)** — ✅ Core conclusion grounded. The "1,459 fires in June 2025" figure comes from the question background rather than independent research (Turkish Minute reports 3,224 total for all of 2025), but the essential point — deaths are rare relative to fire counts — is well-supported.

**Factor 6 (Trend toward more severe seasons, logit=+0.3, conf=0.5)** — ✅ Grounded. 2024 had 12 deaths ~June 21 (in window), 2025 had 3 deaths June 29 (just outside). Correctly characterized.

### Double-Counting
Factors 1 and 3 share overlap: both reference current fire conditions (active fires + strong winds). Factor 1 is about existence of fires, Factor 3 about wind as a death mechanism. Mild overlap but distinct enough to not be pure double-counting.

### Wrong-Direction Factors
None found. All logit signs are consistent with descriptions.

### Contradictory Assessment
None. The 25% estimate aligns with logit math: anchor logit(-1.73) + factor sum(+0.45) = -1.28, implying ~22%. The 25% is slightly above but reasonable.

### Tool Audit
The agent says "Web search found articles about Kundu/Belek fires but dates were ambiguous" — this is accurate. However, the audit somewhat understates the fetch_url error rate (33.3%, 2 of 6 failed with HTTP 403). No factual disagreement with ground truth metrics, but the characterization is rosier than reality.

### Counterargument Quality
Adequate. The counterargument argues the probability should be higher (~35-45%), citing the crisis-level situation and heat pulse timing. It constructs a coherent alternative story, though it relies heavily on the same unverified Antalya fire claims. It does identify the "wet conditions paradox" as a specific mechanism.

### Confidence Self-Assessment
Appropriately calibrated at 0.35. The thin historical data (6 years), inability to verify current fires, and episodic nature of deaths justify low confidence.

### Probability Assessment
My independent estimate: ~20-25%. The 15% base rate is well-derived (1/6 historical, Poisson 11-14%). The upward adjustment for the confirmed heatwave overlap and question-described active fires is reasonable. Even if Factor 1 were entirely removed, probability would be ~19%, still in the same range. The 25% estimate is defensible.

### Anchor Divergence
15% → 25% is a 10pp departure, well within range for the factors presented. No concern.

### Pre-publication Events
Not applicable — the question window (June 18-28) is entirely after the publication date (June 15).

## Verdict Rationale
**WARN** — Factor 1's core claim ("active wildfires near Antalya with hotel and residential evacuations underway as of mid-June 2026") is not independently verified by the research trace. The agent's own research found "no evidence" of such fires. The factor instead relies on the Metaculus question background text. The agent is transparent about this limitation in its thinking, and removing this factor would shift the probability only from ~25% to ~19%, so it doesn't change the forecast direction. But presenting question-description claims as factual evidence in a factor is a methodological weakness worth flagging.
